project_overview.project_name: Health Security Program in Western and Central Africa, Nigeria - Phase II
project_overview.project_development_objective: Increase regional collaboration and health system capacities to prevent, detect, and respond to health emergencies in the Federal Republic of Nigeria
project_overview.beneficiaries: Total population of Nigeria (223 million people) in all 36 states and FCT; estimated 627 million people of AFW region; livestock farmers; regional institutions (ECOWAS/WAHO)

primary_locations: Western and Central Africa region (regional coordination)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - HeSP Nigeria will operate nationwide including areas experiencing persistent insecurity; security challenges noted in multiple states affecting movement of health commodities and staff

key_components.description: Strengthens planning and management of health security resources; prevents and minimizes impacts of zoonoses and AMR through health security governance, One Health coordination, and AMR stewardship programs

Strengthens multisectoral surveillance systems, laboratory networks, and workforce for early detection through collaborative surveillance, laboratory quality improvement, and training programs (FELTP, ISAVET)
Builds capacities for disease control and effective emergency response through emergency management, health service delivery continuity, pharmaceutical regulation, and local manufacturing support

key_components.component_name: Component 4: Program Management and Institutional Capacity
key_components.description: Supports project management, coordination, technical assistance, M&E, and institutional capacity building at national and subnational levels
Provides immediate resources for epidemic/outbreak response or disaster mitigation through rapid reallocation of uncommitted funds upon eligible emergency

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) as primary implementing agency; Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare (FMoHSW); Federal Ministry of Livestock Development (FMLD); Federal Ministry of Environment (FMEnv)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Regional Coordination Committee (RCC) convened by WAHO and CEMAC; National Steering Committee (NSC) chaired by Coordinating Minister for Health; National Technical Committee chaired by NCDC Director General; State Steering Committees (SSC) at state level; National Project Coordination Unit (NPCU) within NCDC; State Project Coordination Units (SPCU) in participating states
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (February 2025); STEP system for tracking; consolidated procurement packages; market engagement; capacity building; multiyear procurement planning; mandatory 30% local labor allocation for international civil works
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: NPCU and SPCUs responsible for M&E; semi-annual progress reports; independent verification agency (IVA) to verify PBC achievement; data sharing platform for regional reporting; results framework with baseline, targets, and collection methodologies

identified_risks.risk_description: Persistent insecurity in multiple areas disrupts movement of health commodities and staff; delays emergency response in high-risk areas; complicates state coordination; Federal-State dynamics and variability in state commitment and capacity; potential political interference in resource allocation; delays in decision-making
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Clearly defined governance and decision-making arrangements in PIM; performance-based contracts; strengthened NPCU/SPCU capacity; transparent procurement and reporting; robust ESF instruments; close coordination with development partners and regional platforms

identified_risks.risk_description: Persistent inflation (33% in 2024), exchange rate volatility, fiscal pressures; health sector reliance on imported pharmaceutical supplies and ICT equipment; limited access to US dollars could disrupt procurement and erode purchasing power
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government policy to industrialize medical value chain; pooled procurement mechanisms; local manufacturing capacities; recently rolled-out social protection programs; support for local pharmaceutical production through PVAC

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex multisectoral and intersectoral collaboration required; deviation from standard siloed sectoral approach; ambitious vision requiring significant coordination and political will; need for sustained investments in health security with One Health focus
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Building on existing multisectoral One Health platforms; appointing strong conveners at regional and national levels; dedicated hands-on support to countries and sectors; regional coordination mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Capacity gaps in FM and procurement; weaknesses in procurement systems; fragmented procurement activities; delays in processes; weak internal controls
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Leverage existing management structures from REDISSE and COPREP; experienced FM staff deployment; robust segregation of duties; compliance with OAGF FM manual; acquisition of accounting software; independent external auditor engagement

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of misuse of funds; fraud and corruption; interference in FM staff deployment; weak capacity of FM staff; weak internal audit function; use of funds for non-eligible expenditures
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Implementation support arrangements; experienced FM staff from FPFMD and PFMUs; strong control environment; robust segregation of duties; compliance with OAGF FM manual; accounting software with IFR generation capability; independent external auditor; designated US dollar and NGN accounts

identified_risks.risk_description: Capacity gaps; procurement technology system weaknesses; splitting of procurement activities into small packages; delays in procurement processes
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD); consolidation of procurement activities; market engagement; procurement capacity building; detailed procedures in PIM; effective monitoring of risk mitigation; multiyear procurement planning

identified_risks.risk_description: Land use issues and exclusion of marginalized populations; community health and safety concerns during construction; inadequate stakeholder engagement; occupational health and safety risks; biohazard and chemical hazards in laboratories; labor risks including SEA/SH; increased energy and water demand; potential use of force by security personnel
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF with risk mitigation procedures; standalone LMP with OHS plan and Code of Conduct; ESCP; SEP; GRM with GBV procedures; HCWMP; SMF; data protection protocols; AI impact assessments; accessibility plans for people with disabilities; targeted outreach and communication campaigns

identified_risks.risk_description: Need for sustained policy commitment; alignment with national health sector reforms
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Alignment with HOPE-PHC and IMPACT projects; support for NHSRII implementation; integration with SWAp; complementarity with other World Bank operations

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential resistance to reforms; need for buy-in from multiple sectors and levels of government
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Stakeholder engagement through SEP; civil society organization platform (Partnership for Amplified Voice); community engagement mechanisms; transparent decision-making processes

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use, and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM developed under COPREP; updated GRM manual to be prepared within 90 days of effectiveness; includes GBV redress procedures; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service) available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Nigeria ranks 130th out of 146 countries on World Economic Forum Gender Gap Index; women comprise most healthcare workforce but underrepresented in technical roles (34.5% of physicians, 22% of STEM graduates); only 26.93% of FETP/FELTP trainees are women; women disproportionately affected during outbreaks (55% of EVD cases, 56% of contacts); project targets 40% women in FETP/FELTP and ISAVET training; gender assessment of IHR capacities; gender-disaggregated surveillance data; flexible training formats; support for women's professional development and leadership

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project operates in areas experiencing persistent insecurity and conflict; security challenges noted as disrupting movement of health commodities and staff, delaying emergency response in high-risk areas, and complicating state coordination; project includes deployment of security personnel to accompany health officers during disease outbreaks in security-prone areas with potential risks of use of force; mitigation through close coordination with development partners and regional platforms to maintain collaboration despite security constraints; no project funds for firearms; security personnel must adhere to Good Practice Note guidelines
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria faces significant fragility factors: (1) Persistent insecurity and conflict in multiple areas; (2) Weak institutions and limited transparency/accountability undermining public trust; (3) Complex federalized structure with 36 autonomous states presenting coordination challenges; (4) Weak human capital and inadequate investments in education and health; (5) Economic challenges including low government health spending (0.62% of GDP), high poverty (54% below poverty line), and macroeconomic instability; (6) Weak health system with poor health outcomes (life expectancy 54 years, under-five mortality 114 per 1,000, maternal mortality >1,000 per 100,000); (7) Multiple concurrent disease outbreaks and endemic infectious diseases; (8) Climate vulnerability and exposure to climate shocks; (9) Governance challenges and perceptions of corruption
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project will operate in areas with persistent insecurity; security challenges affect health worker safety and commodity movement; project includes deployment of security personnel to accompany health officers in security-prone areas; potential risks of use of force; no project funds for firearms; security personnel must follow Good Practice Note guidelines; cross-border collaboration may be hindered by insecurity; regional coordination mechanisms important for maintaining collaboration despite security constraints
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no explicit mention of displacement or refugee populations in the PAD; however, project operates in conflict-affected areas and includes support for Points of Entry (PoEs) surveillance which may involve border areas with potential refugee/migration flows
